videos.title: Measuring Capacity | Litres and Millilitres for Kids
videos.description: Learn about capacity: how much liquid a container can hold. Understand the difference between millilitres (mL) and litres (L) with everyday examples.
learningObjectives: Learn millilitres and litres as units
keyConceptsCovered: Capacity is how much a container can hold

videos.title: Full, Empty, and Half Full | Capacity for Beginners
videos.description: Learn about full, empty, and half full containers. See how we describe how much water or juice is in a glass, bottle, or bucket.
learningObjectives: Describe containers as full, empty, or partly filled
Compare containers by how much they hold
keyConceptsCovered: Full means no more can fit

videos.title: Grams and Kilograms | Measuring Weight for Kids
videos.description: Review how we measure weight in grams and kilograms. See the connection: just like 1 kg = 1000 g, we have 1 L = 1000 mL for capacity!
learningObjectives: See the parallel between weight and capacity units
Understand the 1000 relationship in both systems
keyConceptsCovered: Both systems use the factor of 1000
Weight is for solids, capacity is for liquids

videos.title: Multiplication by 10, 100, and 1000 | Easy Multiplication Tricks
videos.description: Practice multiplying by 1000 to prepare for capacity conversions. If 1 L = 1000 mL, then 3 L = 3 x 1000 = 3000 mL!

excellent_performance.description: For students who scored >= 90% on readiness
excellent_performance.rationale: Quick coverage of capacity concepts for strong students

ready_performance.description: For students who scored 80-89% on readiness
ready_performance.rationale: Complete coverage of capacity with weight-capacity parallel

partial_readiness.description: For students who scored 50-79% on readiness
partial_readiness.rationale: Start with full/empty review, then capacity units

after_prerequisite_review.description: For students who scored < 50% but completed prerequisites

questions.question: Which unit would you use to measure the water in a swimming pool: millilitres or litres?
questions.explanation: A swimming pool holds a very large amount of water, so we measure it in litres. Millilitres are for small amounts like a spoon of medicine.

usageNotes.teacherGuidance: Use real containers in class: a medicine spoon (5 mL), a glass (250 mL), a water bottle (1 L), and a bucket (10 L). Let children pour water between containers to understand capacity.
usageNotes.parentGuidance: Look at labels on bottles and containers at home. Ask your child: How many millilitres in this juice box? How many litres in this water bottle?
usageNotes.studentGuidance: Capacity is about how much liquid fits inside a container. Small amounts like medicine use millilitres, and big amounts like water in a bucket use litres!
